□ *SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814
(916) 445-8360

□ 833 "G" STREET
EUREKA 95501
(707) 444-4816

COMMITTEES:
CHAIRMAN, HEALTH
RESOURCES, LAND USE,
AND ENERGY
WATER
SUBCOMMITTEES:
HEALTH MANPOWER
MENTAL HEALTH AND
DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES
PARKS AND FORESTRY
FISH AND GAME
SELECT COMMITTEE:
HEALTH SCIENCES EDUCATION
JOINT COMMITTEES:
LEGISLATIVE AUDIT
ON FEDERAL SOCIAL SECURITY
AMENDMENTS OF 1972
FAIRS ALLOCATION AND
CLASSIFICATION
BOARDS:
NATIONAL COMMISSION ON
PUBLIC-GENERAL HOSPITALS
ADVISORY HEALTH COUNCIL
WILDLIFE CONSERVATION BOARD
WESTERN STATES FORESTRY
TASK FORCE

Assemblyman
BARRY KEENE

Representing the
Redwood Empire

CHAIRMAN
ASSEMBLY HEALTH COMMITTEE
AND CHAIRMAN
SPECIAL SUBCOMMITTEE
ON
HEALTH CARE INVESTIGATIONS

August 16, 1976

Raymon A. Parnay, Secretary
Forestville Union School District
Board of Trustees
6321 Highway 116
Forestville, California 95436

Dear Mr. Parnay:

Thank you for sending me a copy of the resolution passed
recently by the Forestville Union School District Board of Trustees,
indicating the members' belief that their being required to file
a financial disclosure statement is an infringement on their rights
as private citizens.

Since this requirement stems from the "Political Reform
Act of 1974", I have taken the liberty of sharing your resolution
with Daniel Lowenstein, Chairman of the Fair Political Practices
Commission. And, by copy of this letter, I am asking him to reply
directly to you regarding the continued need for such reporting.

If I can be of assistance with this or other matters, do
not hesitate to contact me again.

Sincerely,

BARRY KEENE

BK:gbj
cc: Daniel Lowenstein with encl.

BOARD OF TRUSTEES

Stanley Anderson
David Ford
Henry Shiblm
Hal Wood
Stan Holley

Forestville Union School District
6321 HIGHWAY 116
FORESTVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95436

August 5, 1976

DISTRICT SUPERINTENDENT PRINCIPAL
Raymond A Purnoy

VICE PRINCIPAL
Robert Christiansen

ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT
Jeffrey Tobias

Ph. (707) 887-2279

At a board of trustees meeting held at Forestville Union
School on August 5, 1976, the following resolution was
passed:

CONFLICT OF INTEREST

WHEREAS, public school boards are required by public law
(Government Code Section 87100 et seq.) to prepare
and adopt a district Conflict of Interest Code, and

WHEREAS, board members of said school boards are required by
law to file a disclosure statement disclosing their
interest and those of their immediate families in
investments, real property, and income, and

WHEREAS, these disclosure statements are by law made available
for public scrutiny, and

WHEREAS, other sections of the Government Code (Section 1090
et seq.) and Title 2, California Administrative
Code (Section 18700 et seq.) and the Education Code
(Section 1174) provide the needed legal control
of board member actions, and

WHEREAS, the California "Political Reform Act of 1974" is
an infringement on the rights of private citizens
and their immediate families as an invasion of
personal privacy of board members and their immediate
families, and,

WHEREAS, this act can require personal expenses for attorney
and accountant fees by board members holding non-
paying elective board positions, and

WHEREAS, compliance with the requirements of this code will en-
courage private investigative snooping by persons
bent on personal vendettas, and

WHEREAS, compliance with the requirements of the California
"Political Reform Act of 1974" by boards will result
in the resignation of untold numbers of board members
through out the state, and

DISTRICT SUPERINTENDENT PRINCIPAL
Raymon A Parnay

VICE PRINCIPAL
Robert Christiansen

ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT
Jeffrey Tobers

Ph. (707) 887 2279

Forestville Union School District
6321 HIGHWAY 116
FORESTVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95436
Page 2

Conflict of Interest

WHEREAS, school districts and communities of the State need
citizens with financial solvency, business acumen,
and personal stability to serve as board members.

IT IS HEREBY RESOLVED by the Board of Trustees of the Forestville Union School District to state publicly its opposition
to the inclusion of elected public school board members within the requirements of the California "Political Reform Act
of 1974" and it is resolved to urge its legislators to take
the necessary action to bring about the changes needed to
exclude school board members from the requirements of the
California "Political Reform Act of 1974".

Respectfully Submitted,

Raymon A. Parnay
Secretary
Forestville Union School District
Board of Trustees

BOARD OF TRUSTEES
Charles Anderson
David Best
Tony Martin
Hal Wood
Stan Holley

FPFC

SEP 17 9 56 AH '76

Forestville Union School District

6321 HIGHWAY 116
FORESTVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95436

September 14, 1976

Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
PO Box 8C7
Sacramento, CA  95804

Dear Mr. Bennett:

Thank you for your thoughtful response to the
resolution of the Forestville Union School District
in opposition to financial disclosure for members of
elected boards and their executive officers and/or
administrators.

I take strong issue with your statement that my
school board's position is "without merit". This pro-
vision of the Political Reform Act has to be considered
as one of the all-time bad pieces of legislation! It
clearly smacks of overkill and over-reaction to politi-
cal corruption at higher levels of government.

Further, it represents a significant intrusion
into the private and professional lives of the very
kind of fiscally responsible individuals who should be
serving the public interest through lay board positions.

As perhaps one of the few remaining politically
conservative school administrators, I can personally
testify to the high degree of unfair political pressure
brought to bear upon me and my family by people of radical
and ultra-liberal political persuasions among my school
community. Petty jealousies and gripes can only be
increased by making public that a school administrator
may have more in the way of material things than do his
political detractors.

I personally classify financial disclosure along
with collective bargaining for school employees as a
furthering of the liberal cause designed to emasculate
the strength of propertied people, a part of the "power
to the people" syndrome, if you will!

(cont. page 2)

DISTRICT SUPERINTENDENT-PRINCIPAL
Raymond A. Parnay

VICE-PRINCIPAL
Robert Christiansen

ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT
Jeffrey Tobes

Ph. (707) 887-2279

BOARD OF TRUSTEES

Charles Anderson
David Basil
Tony Martin
Hal Wood
Stan Halley

DISTRICT SUPERINTENDENT-PRINCIPAL

Raymon A. Parnay

VICE PRINCIPAL

Robert Christiansen

ADMINISTRATIVE ASSISTANT

Jeffrey Tobes

Forestville Union School District

6321 HIGHWAY 116
FORESTVILLE, CALIFORNIA 95436

Ph. (707) 887-2279

Page 2

Already, many responsible, intelligent, community
minded members of lay boards across the state have re-
signed. They feel that their financial holdings are
nobody's business but their own. They further were not
willing to suffer abuse at the hands of petty people
as a result of having to reveal their holdings.

I'm certain there are other and better ways of
handling the problem of avoiding conflicts of interest,
if in fact, this is the true and singular intent of this
legislation. You should be working to accomplish this
goal!

Sincerely yours,

Raymon K. Parnay
District Superintendent-Principal
Forestville Union School District

RAP/mh

STATE CAPITOL
ROOM 4078
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
AREA CODE 916—445-4641

RANDOLPH COLLIER
STATE SENATOR

CHAIRMAN
COMMITTEE ON TRANSPORTATION

California State Senate

September 1, 1976


Board of Trustees
Forestville Union School District
6321 Highway 116
Forestville, California 95436

Gentlemen:

My office has received many letters and resolutions
concerning the provisions of the "Political Reform
Act of 1974," especially as the full financial
disclosure requirements affect individuals interested
in being a member of a local school board, fire
department, or other civic body wherein the individual
receives little or no compensation and is only inter-
ested in contributing his time and energy toward the
objective of better government.

I do not believe that the people, in voting for the
"Political Reform Act of 1974" initiative on the ballot,
intended to set up roadblocks or to discourage people
from wanting to serve on schoolboards, local fair
boards or other similar boards.

I intend to review this area and if corrective action
can be taken through legislation, to introduce such
legislation next year.

Sincerely,

RANDOLPH COLLIER

RC:lm

DEPUTY I.D.#
FINANCE
GOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATION
INSURANCE AND FINANCIAL
INSTITUTIONS
TRANSFORMATION

JOINT COMMITTEES
FARM ALLOCATION AND
WATER RESOURCES, VICE CHAIRMAN
LEGISLATIVE BUDGET
LEGISLATIVE BUILDING SPACE
NOTES, CHAIRMAN
ORGANIZATION AND FINANCING
OF LOCAL GOVERNMENT,
VICE CHAIRMAN

SELECT COMMITTEES
TAXATION OF TIMBER AND
TIMBERLAND
WESTERN STATES FOREST
INDUSTRIES TASK FORCE
